original_abstract:
Designing Pb-free relaxors that simultaneously possess high capacitive energy density ($W_{rec}$) and high storage efficiency ($\eta$) is a critical challenge for advancing pulsed power technologies. While local compositional heterogeneity is known to drive complex polar structures in solid solution relaxors, its potential for optimizing energy storage performance has remained largely untapped. Here, we report an exceptionally high $W_{rec}$ of 15.2 J cm⁻³ coupled with an ultrahigh $\eta$ of 91% in $Bi_{0.5}Na_{0.5}TiO_3$-$BaTiO_3$-based relaxors, achieved by specifically engineering local chemical clustering. 

Using a three-dimensional atomistic model constructed from neutron/X-ray total scattering and reverse Monte Carlo simulations, we identify sub-nanometer clusters of Bi, Na, and Ba. These clusters host distinct polar displacements, confirming predictions from density functional theory. This unique arrangement generates a polar state characterized by small clusters and significant fluctuations in both the magnitude and direction of unit-cell polar vectors. Macroscopically, this translates to enhanced high-field polarizability, minimal hysteresis, and superior breakdown strength. Furthermore, these structural features result in an unusual field-driven increase in efficiency, excellent cycling stability, and high discharge capacity. Our findings demonstrate that hidden local chemical order significantly dictates the polarization dynamics of relaxors and can be strategically utilized to reach new frontiers in energy storage performance.
humanized_abstract:
Pb-free relaxors with high capacitive energy storage wrec and efficiency η are urgently needed for advanced pulsed power applications. While local compositional heterogeneity in the structure of solid solution relaxors is responsible for the complexity of the polar state, its application to energy storage has not been fully explored. In this work, we demonstrate that high wrec = 15.2 J cm−3 and ultrahigh efficiency η = 91% can be simultaneously achieved in a model system of lead-free Bi0.5Na0.5TiO3–BaTiO3 relaxor ceramics by designing local chemical clustering.

Local structural and polar ordering in Ba0.6Bi0.4Ti1-xNxs (BNT) ceramics: Atomic scale investigations The existence of sub-nanometer-sized clusters of Bi, Na and Ba species along with their respective polar displacements have been demonstrated using the three dimensional (3D) atomistic model obtained from neutron/X-ray total scattering and reverse Monte Carlo simulations validated using density functional theory calculations. These structural features are associated with the polar state of relaxors characterized by the existence of small-sized polar clusters and large fluctuations in the magnitudes and directions of unit cell polar vector, which leads to high polarizability at high fields, low remnant polarization and coercive fields, high breakdown electric field, an unusual field dependence of electric current and excellent energy storage characteristics such as low remnant polarization, low coercive field, high breakdown electric field and excellent cycle stability along with high discharge energies, thereby indicating the role of hidden local order in the polarization behavior of relaxors ceramics and their utilization in energy storage applications.

document.input:
Designing Pb-free relaxors that simultaneously possess high capacitive energy density ($W_{rec}$) and high storage efficiency ($\eta$) is a critical challenge for advancing pulsed power technologies.
